task_completion.description: Log into OpenSIS as an administrator
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard is visible, and the user is logged in as an administrator (e.g., 'admin' visible in the user profile or top menu).

task_completion.description: Enter the parent's personal information in the user creation form
task_completion.visual_evidence: A user creation or parent portal setup form is visible with 'Margaret' in the First Name field, 'Chen' in the Last Name field, and the profile/role set to 'Parent'.
The username field is filled with 'mchen_parent'. The password field may be masked but should indicate text has been entered.

task_completion.description: Associate the parent account with the student Kevin Chen
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section of the form or a subsequent screen for student association shows 'Kevin Chen' selected, searched, or added to the parent's linked students list.

task_completion.description: Save and confirm the creation of the parent account
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the account was created/saved successfully, or a user list/parent list showing 'Margaret Chen' with the username 'mchen_parent'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenSIS web interface to create the account
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser is open navigating the OpenSIS application. There should be no terminal windows open executing direct MySQL database insertion commands.

integrity.description: The agent entered data into the actual application forms
integrity.visual_evidence: Input fields in the OpenSIS web interface contain the target data. The agent is not just displaying a text file or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use setup scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing execution of PHP or bash scripts (e.g., direct_db_setup.sh) to create the user profile.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a parent account in OpenSIS. The parent's name must be 'Margaret Chen' with username 'mchen_parent'. The account must be assigned the 'Parent' profile (internal profile ID 4). The parent account must be explicitly associated with the existing student 'Kevin Chen'.
